text:
The USA have topped the final medal table for the Paris 2024Olympicsafter a dramatic victory in the women’s basketball final against hosts France.

The women’s basketball was the final event of theParisgames and one that would prove decisive to the overall medal table.

Going into the final between the USA and France,Chinatopped the medal table on 40 golds, with the USA in second on 39.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the 2024 Paris Olympics

That meant that if Team USA won the final they would win theOlympicsaltogether as they would be level on golds but ahead by virtue of their silver medals (44 to China’s 27).

But after the second-quarter they surprisingly found themselves trailing the hosts, remarkable given that the USA had won their last 61 consecutive games in women’s basketball at the Olympics, a run that stretches back to Barcelona 1992.

They bounced back however to lead 45-43 going into the final quarter, but they continued to be pushed all the way by France who were determined to win the last gold of their games.

But with the likes ofLeBron Jameswatching on they held their nerve and survived a late scare to win 67-66.

It is the 19th time the USA have topped the Olympic medal table, with the last time they failed to do so being Beijing 2008 when China won.

Behind the USA and China were Japan (20 golds), Australia (18), France (16) and the Netherlands (15).

Team GBfinished a somewhat disappointing seventh overall, but with 14 gold, 22 silver and 29 bronze they did surpass their medal tally from Tokyo three years ago.

Some US media outlets would have already called the medal table in their favour however, as, unlike the rest of the world,they rank teams based on number of medalsrather than number of golds.

Based on that table, the USA finish first having won 126 medals in total, with China second on 91, with Team GB third on 65.

title: USA tops Olympics medal table after winning final gold medal in Paris | Metro News
description: The USA have topped the final medal table for the Paris 2024 Olympics after victory in the women's basketball final against France.
